title: Gibbs Free Energy Spontaneity Explorer
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Manipulate enthalpy, entropy, and temperature sliders to trace ΔG vs. temperature, identify spontaneous regions, and locate ΔG = 0 crossover points.

learningObjectives: Analyze how the signs of ΔH and ΔS influence spontaneity at high and low temperatures.
Use ΔG = ΔH − TΔS to determine whether a reaction is spontaneous at a specified temperature.
prerequisites: Comfort working in Kelvin and converting J ↔ kJ

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Interactive plot with guided readouts and presets
keyQuestions: What happens to ΔG when temperature increases for each ΔH/ΔS sign combination?
At what temperature does ΔG change sign for a given reaction?
commonMisconceptions: Negative ΔG automatically means a reaction is fast.
Positive ΔS guarantees spontaneity regardless of ΔH.
assessmentOpportunities: Students record ΔG, TΔS, and spontaneity across three temperatures.
Learners compute crossover temperature from ΔH and ΔS and verify with the visualization.
